reasoning: The time remaining until resolution is very short (from November 9, 2025, to December 31, 2025, or 52 days). Historically, despite decades of high tensions, the Tanker War, and numerous threats, Iran has never fully closed or severely restricted the Strait of Hormuz to international traffic. Doing so would be a massive escalation that would economically devastate Iran (disrupting its own vital oil exports to China) and likely invite a swift and overwhelming direct military response from the United States and its allies. Even with the creator's clarification that interdicting only Western shipping would count, the 52-day timeframe limits the window for such a severe and sustained escalation. Without evidence of an immediate and unprecedented hot war breaking out as of November 2025, the base rate of such an event in any given two-month window is close to zero. Therefore, I estimate a very low probability of 3%.
